popularStory.headline: Memorial
popularStory.description:
Greywelle.
The manor sometime belonged to a certain Reginald de Mohun, to him and his heirs in fee, who gave it to William, his younger son, and the heirs of his body. The said William had two daughte …

zh.Torquay St. John, Devonshire, England: 英國英格蘭戴文托爾華 聖 約翰
zh.lifeSummary: 當Reginald II de Mohun於大約1206年出生在英國薩默塞特登斯特時，他的父親Reginald de Mohun24歲，母親Alice de Briwere24歲。他於大約1225年與Hawise FitzGeoffrey在英國諾福爾可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1258年1月20日在英國英格蘭戴文托爾莫胡享年53歲，埋葬在英國戴文阿可明斯特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Mohun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Moon
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Moyon in La Manche. Compare   Mounce  .
Americanized form of German   Mohn   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

nameMeanings.name: Reginald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Norman origin, derived from Reginaldus, a Latinized form of Reynold influenced by Latin regina ‘queen’. The full form is now regarded as very formal, and bearers generally shorten it to Reg in ordinary usage.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Reginald II de Mohun was born about 1206, in Dunster, Somerset, England, his father, Reginald de Mohun, was 24 and his mother, Alice de Briwere, was 24. He married Hawise FitzGeoffrey about 1225, in Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died on 20 January 1258, in Tormohun, Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 53, and was buried in Axminster, Devon, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Reginald II de Mohun est né environ 1206, à Dunster, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Reginald de Mohun, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Alice de Briwere, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Hawise FitzGeoffrey environ 1225, à Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 20 janvier 1258, à Tormohun, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterré à Axminster, Devon, Angleterre.

Er hat um 1225 in Norfolk, England Hawise FitzGeoffrey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 20. Januar 1258 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Tormohun, Devon, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Axminster, Devon, England begraben.

Lui sposato Hawise FitzGeoffrey circa 1225, nel Norfolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. ko.lifeSummary: Reginald II de Mohun(이)가 대략 1206년 영국 서머세트 던스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Reginald de Mohun(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Briwere(은)는 24세였다. 그는 대략 1225년 영국 노르폴크에서 Hawise FitzGeoffrey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1258년 1월 20일 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 토모헌에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 데폰 액민스터에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Hawise FitzGeoffrey aproximadamente 1225, em Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. ru.Torquay St. John, Devonshire, England: торкуеи ст джон, девон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Reginald II de Mohun родился около 1206 г. в местности дунстир, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Reginald de Mohun было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Alice de Briwere, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Hawise FitzGeoffrey около 1225 г. в местности норфолк, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер 20 января 1258 г. в местности тормохун, девон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 53 лет и был похоронен в местности аксминстир, девон, Англия.
